pattern: Ranks only ever compare cells sharing a row or a column, so processing values from smallest to largest and merging same-valued cells that are row/column-linked into one group lets every group's rank be fixed the moment it is reached -- one pass, union-find per value tier.
hints: Two cells can only constrain each other's rank if they share a row or a column, and equal values in the same row/column must end up with exactly the same rank -- so think of ranking value by value, smallest first.

For a fixed value, cells with that value which share a row or a column belong together: union their row-index and column-index into the same group using a union-find keyed on 'row r' and 'column c' as separate nodes.

Once you know a group of same-valued, row/column-linked cells, its rank must be exactly one more than the largest rank already assigned to any row or column the group touches -- assign that rank to every cell in the group and update those rows'/columns' running maximum before moving to the next value.

edge_inputs.description: four rows three columns with repeated rows
all negative and all equal, must all rank 1
a bridge value links two otherwise separate rows through a shared column
duplicate values not sharing any row or column stay independent

stress.description: random small-range values over a modest grid so many ties and cross-links occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 18))
    n = max(1, min(size, 18))
    matrix = [[rng.randint(-5, 5) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [matrix]
